channel.title: /biz/ Active (filtered, with replies)
channel.description: Filtered out: general, gme, bbbyq. OP + first 3 replies + last 30 replies.

item.title: What made you still hold BTC? — 85 replies — 281.8/hr
item.content:encoded:
What made you still hold BTC?

Open thread • Replies: 85 • Updated: 2026-06-19 22:14 UTC

OP

Bitcoin has gone through multiple cycles, crashes, and endless criticism, yet many continue to hold. What keeps your conviction strong after all these years?

Early replies (first 3)

62371733
>>62371718
>What keeps your conviction strong after all these years?

62371807
The only reason to ever sell bitcoin is to buy more at a lower price.

62371917
>>62371807
correct
>waiting for a good entry

Latest replies (last 30, oldest → newest)

62383588
>>62383366
DYOR on Babylon and you'll see.

62383598
>>62371718
I will still hold it

62383603
>>62382895
How many btc do you hold?

62383619
>>62371718
Sunk cost fallacy. I think I'm retarded. If bitcoin does moon I'm still retarded, but a lucky retard.

62383732
>>62383619
We gonna be rich

62383734
>>62383588
Low apr and kinda not worthy

62383743
>>62375554
Etf is even better

62384166
>>62383734
Sure, it's not life-changing yield, but for some people it's better than letting their BTC sit idle.

62384175
>>62383743
If all you want is price exposure, sure. A lot of people still prefer owning the actual asset.

62384208
Where is satoshi?

62384223
>>62377200
It's all about hype and doesnt have any value

62384235
>>62372468
You better trade memecoins for that

62385618
>>62384235
Go for it. Nobody's stopping you, anon

62386979
>>62384208
Nobody knows

62387122
>>62384166
Do you think it will attract a lot of interest in the long run? The model looks good, and since it's self-custodial while expanding Bitcoin's use cases, I don't think it raises the usual concerns that many people have with DeFi.

62387125
>>62383619
Anon is relaxing

62387534
>>62387122
We'll know for sure once TBV launches on mainnet. That's when we'll see if it gains adoption and makes a real impact.

62387549
>>62383732
If you buy memecoins, not $btc

62387563
>>62387534
Defi is no longer valuable

62387566
Im fine

62387833
>>62387563
Depends what you mean by valuable. A lot of the hype is gone, but people are still borrowing, lending, and farming yield.

62387836
>>62387549
Doge coins lol

62387875
>>62371718
Because it's the only crypto asset I'd be comfortable holding for the next decade without constantly checking the chart.

62387892
the longer this market exists, the more Bitcoin seems to become the benchmark everything else gets compared against. So very very bullish

62388642
>>62387875
100%

62388704
>>62371718
Institutions are starting to make it into BTC, before bridge was the issue but with TBV eliminating that, more money will start flowing into BTC, it still feels early.

62388780
>>62376610
> printing is the only thing keeping the bubble from popping instantly. you want that?

62388819
>>62377444
> nothing has changed except the price chart and your cope levels

62388854
nothing, im not retarded so i take profits. my cost basis is negative now.

62389149
>>62388780
You know my answer for that

item.title: Investing in Sex Robots & Aritifical Womb Technology — 77 replies — 278.6/hr
item.content:encoded:
Investing in Sex Robots & Aritifical Womb Technology

Open thread • Replies: 77 • Updated: 2026-06-19 22:15 UTC

OP

This will be one of the greatest investment opportunities in our lifetime within another decade or so.

Tech is stagnating, just as every other industry is out of viable optimization methodologies. There is nothing new being created, every niche is saturated and optimized. AI will produce some value but it is ultimately a giant bubble that will never, and I mean never advance to the point of consciousness or intelligence (unless they use biological brains and do so correctly - they will fail). Science doesn't even know where consciousness comes.

That being said: sex robots and eventual artificial womb tech (much easier than actually creating consciousness) will be the big new technology that solves real world problems. And the funny thing is: tech companies will eventually run out of other means of generating increasing revenue and will essentially HAVE to go this route, just like they're currently doing with AI. They have nothing else. This technology will have vast and continuous demand as it will entirely replace the old/current paradigm.

Early replies (first 3)

62375864
>>62375553
I want sexbots to be real so bad

62375914
>>62375864
same
all the despair, pain and unhappiness i feel... if it was a good sexbot i would be happy.
the thing is, the first sexbots will be bad. like truly awful. probably not physically dangerous... maybe. but not enjoyable. like how electric masturbators are really good at making me nut, but a soulless experience that leaves me feeling empty. the technology to replace a living organism with a robot is just not there.
if it were viable, there would be news. most people who are lonely and horny want the sexbot. but porn and masturbators are the best we have. sexbots are an AI complete problem.

62375977
>>62375553
>sex robots and eventual artificial womb tech (much easier than actually creating consciousness)
AI is not about creating consciousness. you are retarded if you think intelligence requires consciousness.

Latest replies (last 30, oldest → newest)

62382794
>>62382783
Can I look at your feet?

62382802
>>62382794
no thats gross and gay

62382809
thoughts?

62382812
>>62382802
You're for looking only and you won't even let us look? Fucking prude.

62382814
>>62382774
> new tech would be easily available to public and not only accessible by the wealthy
> the poors and religious fundamentalists wouldn’t continue to multiply like rabbits
Yeah, no more mothers, for sure, good point

62382816
>>62382809
That’s an AI video and very creepy

62382832
>>62376404
Truly horror. The audacity of these humans. It's not just humans they perform horrific and abominable procedures too but animals as well.

>>62377063
Trvke. A horror experiment from decades ago where they removed any sort of affection to a batch of babies. Just mechanical feeding via picking up the kid, no affection, and using a bottle. They quickly started dying off and the experiment ended.

62382834
>>62382814
In the context of "women would still hold their value leveraging this to gain more freedom" and using that as a suggestion of how such women would still hold value...

Yeah nah, the women "leveraging" this to obtain more "freedom" would not be mothers. That wouldn't be the go to source of value to anyone coherent trying to argue birthing was irrelevant to a woman's value. It's ultra retarded.

62382837
>>62375914
Just go somewhere you can hire a prostitute you fucking retard. Then when you realize pussy isn't that special, just buy a quality flesh light and move on with your life.
>>62376404
Thanks Anon, I fully intend to make an entire army of white children this way and your diatribe has made sure I will fight for access during incubation.

62382852
Some of you guys are so fucking stupid that you can not see what is happening right in front of you.

We are long past jerking each other off about the ethics and morality of things....if those ideas had any meaning, we would not allow girls to make more money than a fucking surgeon just by selling her bath water.

OP is fucking right - Robo pussy & artifical wombs is going to fucking send.

This is bitcoin when it was $12.76
This is Amazon when jeff was still living in new mexico selling books
This is Youtube when it had like only 1000 accounts that have registered on the site

I just had an old 55+ year old guy tell me the only reason women have value today is because men want whats between there legs. Throw that out and she's not worth the salt.

The world economic forum stated way back almost a decade ago THAT THIS ALONG WITH GENE-DNA EDITING is going to fucking send.

Think you dumb fuck "If robo pussy is bitcoin at $12.76....what does robo-pussy look like when its at a trillion dollar marketcap?"

62382854
>>62382834
I really want to know what woman ever saw motherhood as a path to freedom. Marriage, yes, but motherhood no.
It’s a desire built into them to want concern, similar to men, but freedom has nothing to do with it. Most women recognize the immediate responsibility and shackling to the child, it’s why motherhood age has increased.

The same women who are trophy wives would continue to be trophy wives because their value was never motherhood.
Haha these threads never cease to crack me up.
Where is this misconception that women lorded babies over men’s heads, and not men lording babies over other men’s heads? They’re the keepers of the babies or some shit not because or an avenue to freedom but because if they pick wrong it’s guaranteed misery

62383573
>>62375553
okay and what stock do I invest in?

62383663
>>62376404
get real, we live in a world where it's legal to kill babies and people call them parasites, trash parents with substance abuse issues poisoning kids before they're even born, most animals are better parents than human mothers, this will be an improvement.

62383804
>>62382448
>when hookers are literally right there and are real women
That's kind of the problem though. They're real women. With everything that goes with that, both good and bad. There's definitely demand for something between porn and sex robots, but I also wonder if there isn't something way more immersive and easier than sex robots possible to deliver. Maybe some type of VR experience, short of having to give Elon Musk control of your brain.

62385644
>>62382448
Depends where you live. In a lot of first world countries hooker are illegal and the ones you'll find are either ugly 40s chicks or they request $800 min for half an hour with them.

62386435
>>62375553
This is a bad idea because governments across the world already have laws coming down the pipe banning these incredibly dystopian technologies.

62386545
>>62375553
Sex robot is my hand. My artificial womb is my hand. I don't need to buy poorly made overpriced plastic shit made by jews to cope with my lonelyness

62386576
>>62386545

>Lonely
>Can't afford hookers
>Blames Jews

62386645
>>62386576
I can afford your mom but I grew tired of prostitutes over the years. Too much hassle, choosing a girl, making an appointment, get to her place, only to see a slampig whore reeking of pizza, fuck that. I'd rather watch some amy rose futa banging on rouge the bat and be done with my urges

62386707
>>62375553
I think you’re directionally correct but wrong about the details. I don’t see a huge market for artificial wombs in isolation, it’s not a lack of wombs that are leading to our societal problems, it’s that women as a whole are retards when it comes to shepherding their most valuable commodity: their eggs. So we would need an adjunct tech like IVG for our species to really transcend past a primitive state.

62387048
This thread is a great example of why /biz/ will never make it.

Sexbots/AI romantic companions are already a thing, and its a growing muliti-billion dollar industry. The sexdoll industry is also growing and is highly competitive on the higher end. One of the few cases where AI might actually be what its hyped up to be. All attraction is "coded", whether by evolution or by actual code.

Artificial wombs directly target the growing surrogacy market, where even in cheap jurisdictions like Columbia, the average surrogate agency charges $80k or more. US is more like $200k. This is prime territory for an artificial replacement. The single dad question is already being answered by the surrogacy industry, and their is increasing YoY demand from single men to become fathers.

What I would need to see is some kind of local AI capable of running the bot's mind and the body. It will likely cost $50k in about 10 years just to build the brain that can run the bot and run the brain. The bot will be expensive, but it could be financed on a 10 year term like lots of other luxury goods like exotic cars, boats and other shit. Or it can be part of a worker's compensation package for working in a remote environment like the Arctic for long periods of time.

62388341
>>62386645

>Can only afford fat ugly hookers
>Jerks off to futa faggotry
>Blames Jews

62388354
>>62375553
Milfs owe me sex.

There are male pornstars like Jordi el Niño Polla, Juan el Caballo Loco, Ricky Spanish, Jodie Johnson, Elias Cash, etc. That get to have sex with milfs all day while incels wageslave to death in the trades.

Milfs owe me sex for all the suffering I've had to endure.

62388392
>>62387048
>The single dad question is already being answered by the surrogacy industry, and their is increasing YoY demand from single men to become fathers
what? You mean they're paying single dads to become fathers?

62388813
>>62388341
You made me laugh, anon kek. Girls post fake photos, edited photos, or real photos that were taken like 20 years. There is this thing called filters, what can a man do except to stumble the same rock over and over out of horniness? It's not a problem of money believe me, I could afford "vip" prostitutes but when you go to do the needful to their places you end up in bed with a fat old whore with a ton of make-up. I'd rather fap to sissy hypno amy rose futa cock (and ballsack) cumming hands free shit and be done with it

62388898
>>62388392
No. I mean single fathers are a growing percentage of surrogacy clients.

62388998
>>62388898
Yeah I wonder how this will work long term for the species. Birth rates plummeting everywhere and no country seems to have a solution. Outside of total political/societal collapse and semi-enslaving women as they were throughout most of human history I don’t see the problem resolving. That only leads to completely severing male/female cooperation from reproduction where a man or woman can have children independently. No idea how a child will fare without two parents either. And what will become of remnant women if their ability of reproduction becomes redundant thanks to new tech? Would anyone voluntarily choose to have more women than men? The only edge they have over the male sex is reproduction, but men by and large will win at every competition of intellect or athletics. So what do you do with females if they become obsolete in the future? I think best case scenario you could selectively breed them or genetically engineer them to be lovable companions, like dogs are today, but ones where you can have sex and be romantically intimate with. That’s assuming we retain semblance of a dimorphic species in the future

>>62388813
If they don’t look like their pics then just walk out and don’t pay them lol. No one’s forcing you to shag a ham beast kek

62389106
>>62388813

>Jerks off to futa porn
>Can't even find a decent hooker
>Blames Jews

You're definitely not a faggot anon, my bad. Cumming to a cock and balls is what straight men do, don't let anyone tell you otherwise.

62389112
>>62389106
Sits outside waits for cock. Instead of going to cock

62389152
There’s a bitch I’m fucking now that for like 6 years wouldn’t give me the time a day. I hadn’t messaged her much in the past year or two… and I started messaging her that she looks like the perfect sex robot. She’s like wtf? But then I kept calling her that and she started to like it and now I’m fucking her.

item.title: >govt collects tax >its not enough >issues bonds providing interest for money >needs more money next year >tax isnt enou… — 29 replies — 116.0/hr
item.content:encoded:
>govt collects tax >its not enough >issues bonds providing interest for money >needs more money next year >tax isnt enou…

Open thread • Replies: 29 • Updated: 2026-06-19 22:31 UTC

OP

>govt collects tax
>its not enough
>issues bonds providing interest for money
>needs more money next year
>tax isnt enough
>issues bonds providing interest for money
>did this so many times now need debt to pay back debt

why not just NOT spend like a high end escort?

Early replies (first 3)

62379039
>>62379035
OP is a bulgarian faggot who spams this board and scams people.

62379041
>>62379039
im black and i dont spam this board

62379042
>>62379035
>why not just NOT spend like a high end escort?
the austerity necessary to balance the budget would cause an armed revolt.

Latest replies (last 30, oldest → newest)

62379044
>>62379035
>>62379042

62379046
>>62379035
If you are referring to the US, one of the reasons for this is there was a greater appetite for dollars outside the US than in it. However, things have changed since the dollar system was politicized.

62379058
I want the fed to raise rate to 35% and destroy the world.

thoughts?

62379071
>>62379041
worse, kys nigger

62379101
money exists to be spent. if you don't spend, there are no jobs, there is no development, there are no goods and services. the purpose of an economy is to produce things, not to hoard money. we can already see that the "free market", left to its own devices, prefers to hoard rather than spend, and uses hoarded resources to oppress and stifle growth rather than encourage it

62379304
>>62379044
Hmm those corporate taxes seem kinda low

62380467
>>62379035
Until jfk, we didn't have a deficit (he was the first one to cut taxes for economic growth). Even then, it took until Reagan for tax cuts to turn into a massive problem, with him basically halving them and double national debt in his time. Trump did the same thing (rich nigga tax cuts) and it tripled national debt, then that problem doubled under biden. The government has been collecting more of your taxes and less taxes of the people who actually have money to foot the bill. This shit is what caused the french revolution; king louis said, "we're broke dead fucking poor. Nobles, foot the bill". The Nobles said, "BITCH I'D RATHER DIE" and then they did.

We're already stripped every neccesary and not neccesary government organization and service to the bone. We can't even have free-tax filing; the problem isn't cost, it's revenue.

62384846
>>62379035
The money printer is the opiate of the state. The planet is addicted to the Rothschild smack.

62385524
>>62379304
>taxes
>too low
Go glow somewhere else
Instead how about we stop giving all our fucking money away in foreign aids and dem programs while removing all income taxation

62386402
>>62385524
We can have social services AND have lower taxes for the average person. Just put back corporate tax rates back to what they were during the boomer's time and simplify the tax code.

62386422
>>62385524
Foreign aid is basically nothing. The biggest expenditures are Social Security, Medicare, Medicaid, and VA benefits. Basically everything else is a rounding error.

62388264
>>62386402
>just increase taxes
No, they're already beyond exorbitant
We don't need all these social services just to import the difference and make everybody worse off
>>62386422
>basically nothing
Good, then it won't be missed when it gets cut
>social security
>medicare
>medicaid
Can all be cut as well. We don't need these funds simply to be "borrowed against" to collateralize even more bullshit the government doesn't need to be involved in.
>inb4 oh noes what about the nigger riots!?
Arrested and put to work. 2 for 1
>va benefits
Are just about the only legit thing there, since its upholding the bargain of a citizen giving their life and livelihood to the state for a time in return for perks later in life
I long for the day the state has outward facing teeth instead of inward again

62388492
>>62388264
Do you want a functional society, or is sucking billionaire dick JUST THAT NOURISHING for you? Social services are what separates first-world countries from 3rd world countries. If you want to be live like you live in guam, move to guam. I want to live like I'm european, why don't you?

62388495
>>62379035
because it's easy to spend other people's money. also people get elected by promising to give people free shit. there's no long term thinking or accountability after that.

62388514
>>62388492
>wanting less intrusive bullshit and welfare queens is LITERALLY SUCKING BILLIONAIRE DICK
I hate the tier of disingenuous bullshit the dialog has sunk to on the modern internet
I said what I said and didn't say what your tiktok rotted brain thinks I said
I don't want to live like impoverished, overtaxed euros. I want to live unfettered on my land and tell the government to kindly go fuck itself until I want their involvement, like an American
And you can go live in europe if you think its so much better. Funny how free association works, yeah?

62388519
>>62379044
>Retarded infographic designed by retarded overpaid infodesigner that communicates fuckall except OOHHH PRETTY SHAPES AND COLORS!!
Its over

62388527
>>62388264
you reek of low IQ

62388541
>>62388514
Trust me, I'm trying every day to move. You don't have to worry about me. Either way, yuo said retarded bullshit, and I told you its logical conclusion. Just because you say dumb things and expect other people to take your mistruths as face value doesn't mean I'm going to do the same. It's not the euros struggling to afford to get to work. It's not the euros being bankrupt from a single broken leg. It's not the euros who work more, eat worse, have their kids stuck inside the home in suburbia. It's not the euros paying private companies to pay their own fucking taxes. So you calling THEM impoverished caps your iq at 90, sorry to tell you.
You wanna tell the govt to go fuck itself, but you won't tell the corps? Power is going to centralize somehow, and you can either have some input on that, or let someone not at all beholden to you get the reins. If you want unregulated corps using wood chips to add weight to bread and chip bags or continue to put ethanol in gasoline, do nothing for the latter and wait 10 years for the former.

62388611
>>62388527
>if you disagree with endless money printing, gibs, and importation of incompatible cultures you must be stupid
Wow, what a brilliant and original retort
You may as well have resorted to saarposting
>>62388541
>if you disagree with endless money printing, gibs, and importation of incompatible cultures you must be stupid, long version
Its all so tiresome
I'm not reading all that commie bullshit
Leave and don't let the door hit your fat lazy ass on the way out

62388828
>>62388611
I didn't even talk about immigration wtf?
yfw:

62388844
>>62379035
Enough is an illusion. All want is more

62388858
>>62379035
That's what inevitably happens when you have a monopoly on violence called the state.
You are now in the decline phase of the ponzi scheme, also called end stage statism.
Enjoy slavery :D

62389076
>>62379046
>the dollar system was politicized
qrd?

62389082
>>62380467
If the USA spent nothing for a year or two we could pay back the debt (not that this is remotely feasible). Personally, I like the idea of a sovereign wealth fund intended explicitly for paying the debt back, not sure why Trump axed it when his admin proposed it.

62389166
>>62388264
>No, they're already beyond exorbitant
Are major American companies actually going to pack their bags and leave to another country if gubment starts giving them serious taxes? Playing devil's advocate here, but if the entire world goes to shit, it takes something special for America to outdo the rest of the world due to reserve currency status. Besides, corporations purposefully underpay and expect you to figure out the rest, with retail expecting you to supplement your income with welfare. The welfare state has been a disaster for the poor and the corporations, both create market distortions due to ease of spending and club exclusivity. I'm "middle class," but you better believe if I got the same healthcare and food stamps like the corporations/poor, I would have extra money for saving/consumption.

62389187
>>62379101
Investments aren't hoarding, genius

item.title: There are people in USA who make $51k a year on welfare and live in low cost states and like to larp as middle class. — 16 replies — 64.0/hr
item.content:encoded:
There are people in USA who make $51k a year on welfare and live in low cost states and like to larp as middle class.

Open thread • Replies: 16 • Updated: 2026-06-19 22:30 UTC

OP

There are people in USA who make $51k a year on welfare and live in low cost states and like to larp as middle class.

When did everyone start caring about class suddenly?

Early replies (first 3)

62389137
People don't care about class enough
The working class isn't united, but the Epstein class certainly is.

62389142
>>62389137
>epstein class
Poor leftist redditor liberal detected
You are not owed a job btw.

62389144
>>62389142
At least regime shills in communist countries get a pittance for the efforts, you do it for free

Latest replies (last 30, oldest → newest)

62389146
>>62389129
never show ur true power level nigger never

62389153
>>62389146
What does this have to do with my post?
There are many people who make $50k in usa and claim to be “middle class” or their “$100k income household is middle class.”
They aren’t hiding or showing anything.

62389158
I have a white-collar job which only pays 44k

62389160
>>62389146
The American average income is $65k and even in very expensive states, it is still $65k.
Some npc saying he makes $50k or $50k in welfare isn’t that surprising.
Also, him coping by saying $50k is middle class isn’t surprising either.

62389161
>>62389153
We are all working class, cease the squabbles

62389162
>>62389129
nope

welfare in the US is based on income, and the only way you're making $51k per year and getting welfare is if you have like 15 kids.

for a single adult I think the income limit is around $12k per year to get any government assistance.

welfare is not enough to live on, and you only get it if you're so broke you can't survive.

62389167
>>62389129
if you're saying they make $51k/year FROM welfare that's also bullshit. Welfare in general pays less than a thousand a month. Disability or whatever

if they're making more than $800 per month on disability it is BECAUSE THEY PAID FOR IT. It's from their FICA contributions, not welfare.

62389170
>>62389129
you're probably mistaking insurance we pay for for welfare

Unemployment insurance and disability insurance are things you pay for. Not welfare.

62389172
I can feel my brain leaking out of my ears from the banality of all your post topics OP

62389176
>>62389161
No, the ones doing actual valuable work are working class. The cunty dregs trying to tear that work down are definitely not in the same class.

62389177
>>62389172
traffic is down again. OP is posting as much as he can but not driving engagement. Probably because he's an annoying fuckwad.

62389178
>>62389167
>>62389162
Or VA or some nonsense like that.
>>62389161
What squabble?

62389185
>>62389178
yep, VA might do it, but VA disability is paid for by the employee and employer just like civilian disability.

Not welfare. He paid for that.

item.title: Tick tock ADA-fags — 13 replies — 52.0/hr
item.content:encoded:
Tick tock ADA-fags

Open thread • Replies: 13 • Updated: 2026-06-19 22:32 UTC

OP

Time's almost up.

Early replies (first 3)

62387098
>>62387094
This is now peak shitcoin measurement.

62387348
>>62387094
My bags are light because they have no value

62387364
>>62387094
ADA to $20 EOY

Latest replies (last 30, oldest → newest)

62387467
>>62387364
20c is doable

62387503
Little Charlie showed his true colors by taking a timeout when everything was going down. Is the price of the token truly all that matters to him? What about the giant ecosystem he was building?? top lel

62387504
>>62387503
Atleast Charles is honest. Segey is a sleazy Russian scammer who's been dangling carrots for 9 years. Hopefully he gets lynched by a baggie

62388063
>>62387503
The 900$ total usdt liquidity?

62388092
>>62387094
LINK flippin ADA will be the first step to redemption for the crypto market, ultimately ending with LINK flipping XRP. Only then will cryptofags stop being forsaken.

62388256
>>62387467
in zimbabwean dollars, yes, i agree

62388308
>>62388092
I used to think that ADA and XRP money would jump ship to LINK.
Now I think that when ADA and XRP eventually crashes and burns, the money will simply exit crypto entirely instead.

62388345
>>62388308
Crypto was a liquidity trap from 2016-2022,2023ish

AI is the new hotness and at least produces something somewhat tangible

62388355
>>62387094
lmao they're both slipping out of page 1.
in 10 years we'll have the same screenshots we do from 2014 with dogshit like dash, and other btc forks, but this time with LINK and ADA together, as they both belong to the same tier.

62389188
>>62388092
>LINK flipping XRP. Only then will cryptofags stop being forsaken.
https://link-flip.alwaysbeshipp.ing/
Rent free baggie

item.title: >he went all in on everything except the only investment that matters Why didn't YOU invest in your family? — 23 replies — 36.6/hr
item.content:encoded:
>he went all in on everything except the only investment that matters Why didn't YOU invest in your family?

Open thread • Replies: 23 • Updated: 2026-06-19 21:54 UTC

OP

>he went all in on everything except the only investment that matters

Why didn't YOU invest in your family?

Early replies (first 3)

62379163
you're asking why I didn't invest in my family...

and I will ask in return...

Is killing niggers free and open-source?

62379166
I personally view having kids an essential life goal.
Just give me more time.

62379173
>>62379160
I'm 29 with $300k I'm gonna get my gf pregnant at 30. Hopefully I'll have 400k by the

Latest replies (last 30, oldest → newest)

62379191
>>62379160
I'm trying, bro.

62379204
>>62379166
>I just need to complete this massive list of things that likely won't happen before I start a family

the equivalent of waiting for a big price dip in a good stock that you subconsciously know isn't going to happen

62379219
>>62379160
Bad ROI and infinite P/E.

62379223
>>62379204
Having kids is like being a cuck in a relationship. You watch from the sidelines as others fullfill their lives while you wait your sloppy seconds.

62379248
>>62379223
Lol

62379259
Next bullrun when I'm 40-something I'll find a prime 25 year old girl to marry and have children with.

It's not too late r-right?

62383378
>>62379259
No, but you'll have to accept that she only likes you for money

62383390
>>62379259
You are an old hag, it is so creepy

62383494
>>62379160
What makes you think I didn't?

62383537
>>62379160
You might be able to get a submissive 25yo trad chink wife lining up for you in year 2000 to year 2010 where chink is literally a sinkhole stone age tarzan living in the cave or on top of the tree, that have zero exposure to internet other than brick games. Today you would be lucky to get a brown shitskin 25yo to want to talk to you.

62387425
>>62379160
>Why didn't YOU invest in your family?
Simple, bad ROI

62387541
>>62379160
>have sex incel
>biz edish

62387661
>>62379160
Generally I'm happy alone and I need alone time. Plus I never really fell in love again after 25. After my 3rd big crush the feelings kind of disappeared.

62387677
>>62379223
This sounds like waging, lol

62387680
What family?

62389092
>>62379160
Anon... you didn't have to TRVKE me like this...

62389110
>>62379160
I tried so many times. Females aren't right these days, I tell you wHat.
t. literal white haired Anon

62389116
>>62389110
Imagine going to the frail white shrink before running to the huge cock bull. You make no sense. Scared.

62389121
>>62379160
Went bald. Lost confidence. Dating is no longer viable.

62389122
>>62389116
Oy vey
I was just making a joke, goyim.
Remain relaxed

item.title: Canadian Mortgages are insane — 25 replies — 32.5/hr
item.content:encoded:
Canadian Mortgages are insane

Open thread • Replies: 25 • Updated: 2026-06-19 21:46 UTC

OP

Anyone that bought homes over the last 10 years has no concept of the magnitude of the level of debt they have.

Imagine 400k to 900k mortgages on cookie cutter homes with no space between them. This is just fine in the mind of the average leaf.

Let me be clear, they are never going to pay it off. Never. They think because everyone else has a big ass mortgage that its fine. It took me 17 years just to pay down my 228k mortgage to 68k where it is now. How the fuck do these people think at 38 with a 500k mortgage they can pay it down by the time they retire? LMAO.

Fucking totally disconnected from reality. Millions of us.

Early replies (first 3)

62382768
im sick of this being a thing, just fucking crash already. I have to deal with crashes 100 times a year as a crypto cuck but everyone else in the world with their houses and their gay stocks is just driving off into the sunset. make it make sense nigger

62382788
>>62382765
their retirement plan is your pic

62382793
>>62382788
I find this pick really depressing because it reminds me of my parents. They are just in another fucking world. You cannot convince them that anything is wrong.

Latest replies (last 30, oldest → newest)

62382840
>>62382768
Probably because houses and companies have value while crypto is useless and does nothing at all

62382853
>buy bitcoin 2015
>???
>buy house

what was so difficult?

62382958
>>62382765
The sad thing is that the pod in the pic related doesn't even have to say "MAiD" on it for everyone to know what it's supposed to be.
>>62382788
I'm convinced MAID is some kind of scam to prevent wealth transfer from boomers to their kids; there's no way it could enjoy as broad support amongst boomers (leaf boomers are the most selfish on the planet) if that wasn't the case.
Probably the idea is spend everything, leave nothing, and jump in the death pod when you're tapped out.

62383964
>>62382765
They didn't plan to ever pay it off, inflation eats away at it and they'll roll it into their next house.

Or at least that was the plan before the bubble popped and they all went $200k-500k underwater.

62383966
400K CAD is something like 250K EUR

62383978
Just rent?

62383992
>>62383966
The median household income in canada is like 40k euro. People have no idea how fucked this country is, they're still going off of marketing from 20 years ago.

62384000
>>62383992
I know i was working remotely for a company in Quebec and we all got laid off 3 months ago

62384029
>>62382768
They won't let it crash. BC has gotten rid of all businesses since it's bad for environment and were living on the housing market and US tech sector. Now both is gone so fed need to bail them out. We've already seen them changing the laws so you can't force sell because you're missing mortgage.
No wonder Alberta had enough.

62384087
The market was supposed to crash in 2023/2024, but the government flooded the country with immigrants to keep prices high

62384116
>>62383964
Did it pop?

62384139
>>62382765
Paying it down makes you poor.

62384170
>>62382765
Housing-as-investment needs to crash and die off permanently. So much wealth and productivity is wasted on paying for retardedly overpriced nonproductive shitshacks. Pricegouging wooden boxes just kills the rest of the economy and makes people stop giving a fuck about anything.

62384294
>>62384170
Any kind of land-as-investmen needs to be regulated to hell. There's downtown office space in Toronto that has been sitting empty for YEARS. You're telling me no one wants to open a business in downtown Toronto in YEARS? Fuck land hoarders.

62386762
>>62384170
My wife does corporate taxes. You should see the rent bills and property taxes that small businesses have to pay. Businesses cannot grow here for this reason unless you are mega rich or a megacorp..

62386907
>>62382765
>400k to 900k CAD
So what? 80k in real money? Not so bad

62386920
>>62382788
Rich people will always be the first to kill themselves

62387148
>>62386907
Yeah he's full of shit. Even in bumfuck nowhere it's at least 2x as much for a shitty roach infested closet.
So 160k in real money. Not too bad, but then again so bad it's still not worth it.

62387156
>>62382765
This is why I laugh when people say that home prices in USA are going to stabilize or go lower due to (reduced?) demand. We have a lot further to run and I won't be surprised if the average home price doubles in 10 years.

62387162
>>62382765
Theyre DINKs and youre an incelnigger.

62389093
>>62384170
The government wont let it Mark Carney is gonna remove the foreign buyer ban. So REITs, Blackrock and Blackstone can come in to buy up all the condos and houses that are not selling.

https://www.bloomberg.com/news/articles/2025-12-22/canada-weighs-opening-its-housing-market-to-more-foreign-capital

62389108
>>62389093
They can buy them all they want, they'll lose money every month without an increasing population. Houses aren't like stocks, they're a money pit of taxes, maintenance, insurance and security. They can't afford to hold onto empty houses.

item.title: Is the US and or global economy due for a correction or crash? — 8 replies — 32.0/hr
item.content:encoded:
Is the US and or global economy due for a correction or crash?

Open thread • Replies: 8 • Updated: 2026-06-19 22:28 UTC

OP

I understand these circumstances don’t match the criterion of previous corrections and crashes…
Record inflation, housing affordability, dot-com levels of euphoria, fuckin add to the list while you’re in here.
Thank you in advance faggots

Early replies (first 3)

62388696
>>62388660
It has been for years but money printers go brrrrrrrrr

62388709
>>62388660
Everyone but EU and Japan will pretty much get fucked, especially the US and China.

62389118
>>62388660
We don't have record inflation though, and it's not even close. We had 15% inflation for most of the 1970's.

Latest replies (last 30, oldest → newest)

62389127
>>62388660

SpaceX. Michael Saylor.

62389133
>>62389118
you are absolutely right. sorry. i was talking out of my ass

62389140
>>62388660
its only going to go up in US market cuz muh semiconductor nigger
go all in on DRAM nigger
it will never go down nigger

62389155
>>62389140
it's gotta face a correction eventually... unless theres another booming sector rotation like space

62389183
Nobody has seen him because of cunt and her family. even cunt friends & cousins miss him. The sisters and mom are nothing but bat shit crazy spanish trouble.

item.title: >Be me >Have mild Chrons >Live in Europe >Can arbitrarily choose days to work and stay home and chill >If an employer do… — 8 replies — 32.0/hr
item.content:encoded:
>Be me >Have mild Chrons >Live in Europe >Can arbitrarily choose days to work and stay home and chill >If an employer do…

Open thread • Replies: 8 • Updated: 2026-06-19 22:22 UTC

OP

>Be me
>Have mild Chrons
>Live in Europe
>Can arbitrarily choose days to work and stay home and chill
>If an employer doesn't like this I can sue and make bank

Does this work in the US or not

Early replies (first 3)

62385113
>>62385106
If you list it on your application then they have to accommodate you, but they likely just won't hire you in the first place

62385214
Do you have to prove it to your employer or can people just pretend to have a diarrhea disease?

62385415
>>62385214
It's formalized through the state medical thing.
>>62385113
Then you can sue for descrimination

Latest replies (last 30, oldest → newest)

62385424
>>62385214
you have to shit your pants in the interview in front of the hiring manager

62386358
>>62385106
>>If an employer doesn't like this I can sue and make bank
Not how European worker's rights laws work, amerikun. Employers need to ensure that your work isn't lowering your health or compensate you for it (in massages, working place adjustments or curative benefits), this means that if you employer can ensure you got a crohn's friendly work environment at work he can demand you go to work unless you currently have diarrhea. If this happens multiple days in the row he can demand documentation by a doctor. This is the case for all sicknesses.
>Does this work in the US or not
Take a guess

>>62385113
In some European countries you're legally not allowed to mention it before you're hired.

62389100
>>62385424
kek

62389105
>>62389100
Still want my fridge?

62389169
>>62385424
Sounds easy enough.

item.title: When is the fucking Ass&Piss500 crashing so I can get slurpies? — 7 replies — 28.0/hr
item.content:encoded:
When is the fucking Ass&Piss500 crashing so I can get slurpies?

Open thread • Replies: 7 • Updated: 2026-06-19 22:28 UTC

OP

When is the fucking Ass&Piss500 crashing so I can get slurpies?
BOJ already hiked to 1% interest this week and I was hoping that certain stocks in the Nikkei225 would sink, BUT THEY CLIMBED HIGHER! Then I thought the carry trade Yen/Usd would fuck up the Ass&Piss, also hoping for America to increase interest rate, BUT ITS STILL FUCKING CLIMBING.
Even with the Hormuz straight ITS STILL FUCKING CLIMBING.
REACHING ALL TIME HIGHS.

How? This makes no sense!

Early replies (first 3)

62387100
>>62387085
At this point im demoralized enough by clown markets and clown world to just yolo in. Ive been liquid for 6 months. Bout to give up

62387103
At this point im demoralized enough by clown markets and clown world to just yolo in. Ive been liquid for 6 months. Bout to give up

62387112
>>62387085
the best strategy, in a rigged clown market, is to simply layer in across time instead of across events. you will always be waiting for the big one to hit with a pile of cash sidelined, while the government does literally everything in its power to ensure retirement line goes up. they will fail of course, as they always do, but you will not succeed in timing it perfectly. if you buy across time you can ensure you have at least some capital allocated in the dips, instead of none at all, waiting for a megadip to come when a superdip only ever comes.

Latest replies (last 30, oldest → newest)

62387201
>>62387085
>When is the fucking Ass&Piss500 crashing
it already did bro
you didn't buy?

62387217
hoLY SHIT .25% RATE INCRASE SToP ALL INVESTENT STOP BUYNG SELL EVERYTHING IGNORE AI IGNORE AI IGNORE ROBOTICS 25 BASISPOINTS DONTCHAKNOW

62387219
holy SHIT IGNORE THE STEAM ENGINE THE LORD IS LEVYING .25% MORE GRAIN

LOST DECADE INCOMINIG

62389181
JUST TWO MORE WEEKS

item.title: Imagine letting the government control crypto instead of just being an XMR chad — 6 replies — 24.0/hr
item.content:encoded:
Imagine letting the government control crypto instead of just being an XMR chad

Open thread • Replies: 6 • Updated: 2026-06-19 22:23 UTC

OP

Imagine letting the government control crypto instead of just being an XMR chad

Early replies (first 3)

62387952
>>62387893
I'M ON TEAM CLARITY!!!

62388601
>loser coin

62388699
>>62388601
>loser coin
>top 20 while being delisted from nearly everywhere
more like absolute fucking chad main character coin

Latest replies (last 30, oldest → newest)

62389032
the problem is legality, it always was. if it becomes problematic or illegal to purchase goods with XMR; it doesn't matter if it's good or not.

62389138
Pump and dump incoming, screen cap this

62389171
>>62387893
>dismantle the state
My ass. You’re not dismantling shit. Whether it’s banning it, 51% attacking it, shilling zec as competition and attention diversion, or just shorting the ever loving fuck out of the coin, XMR will not be allowed to gain any traction.

The state WILL destroy it with the power of the money printer or other means. They have written papers about it. Monero will be allowed to be some niche internet drug market but nothing more.

You won’t dismantle the state by sitting on your ass and downloading mining software, sorry. Need some……..other hardware for that

item.title: How is your career going? — 76 replies — 65.1/hr
item.content:encoded:
How is your career going?

Open thread • Replies: 76 • Updated: 2026-06-19 21:22 UTC

OP

I make $28/hr as a Fire Alarm Inspection Technician. I have 2 licenses and 4.5 years of experience:
-FASA/BASA
-NICET Inspection and Testing Level 2

Early replies (first 3)

62381055
Going for the NICET Fire Alarm Systems Level 1 license in the next 2 months or so. Will hopefully get to $30/hr.

62381060
>>62381049
*chirp*

62381078
>>62381049
is that a fun job? sounds comfy desu

Latest replies (last 30, oldest → newest)

62383481
$48 per hour? Thats $2000 per week. You can buy otm SPY contracts and double your weekly net. The issue is. Nobody want to work learn a life changing skill outside of their job because theyre so mentally exhausted from everyday distraction & life. We call ourselves the 1%'ers

62383500
>>62381049
late 20's, 120K in business analytics. It's an adult daycare but the pay's good, feels bad going in the office once a week tho. There are some qts around but that's just about all my social interaction for the week. My dream is to keep the same amount of pay but work in another country in another time zone, but that would be rough. Seriously thinking about it one day

62383513
i make $130k base + 15% bonus target as a corporate accounting manager. i'm underpaid by like $20k/yr but i'm fully remote so i accept it. kind of wish i had done law school instead.

62383520
>>62381049
Just bombed a senior product manager and coordinator interview.
I talk too much and don’t get to the point. That’s the direct feedback I got, which is true.

I have another offer I already accepted at a faang company though. It’s lower level and need to move though.

62383530
>>62383520
>It’s lower level and need to move though.
are you going to do it? i can't imagine moving for work in the year 2026, when these big indianjewnigger companies can (and do) blindly lay people off all the time.

62383536
>>62381049
100% stuck at a job I hate that boring as fuck most days and the few days where I actually need to work I bust my ass off all day because my retarded managers will fuck the schedule up and have only one person on to do three peoples worth of work. I lucked into a middle management position though in a field with basically no mobility so I can't really leave without taking a huge paycut. Make $36/hr which feels like nothing because I also live in a pretty expensive area.

62383538
>>62383096
Whatever you want anon. Start a business on the side or smthg.
>>62383530
I will because can’t take the current company anymore or I will Elliott Rodger someone in Minecraft.

62383574
>>62382822
Commerical and residential buildings in my state literally cannot be occupied if they don't pass a fire inspection

62383585
I deliver mail for 30 bucks an hour. Paid for 8.5 hours usually done in 5-6, it's a weird evaluated quazi salaried thing.

62384021
>>62381049
Early 40s, trapped in a flyover shithole making $80k in data analytics. Entry level title with a senior-level workload and enough experience to at least land interviews for data engineering roles with some regularity, though there are only a handful of openings in my area each year. I'm pretty frugal and on track to be able to semi-retire in Asia within a few years which is looking increasingly likely given the state of the job market and my career.

62384045
i'm a family doc and make ~130/hr. it sucks but at least my schedule is condensed into 4 days a week and i'm hoping to retire in 9 years.

62384057
>42
>middle manager but my job is going to be outsourced in the next year or so
>110k before taxes
>live in New York suburbs so it’s actually not as much money
I have a lot invested and saved so when I’m axed it’s tempting to just try to survive on that instead of working again. no family to support and I inherited the house I live in with no mortgage.

62384184
>$37/hour salary equivalent post taxes and $1k a month 401k contribution
is this good

62384198
>>62384184
Depends on CoL in your area, but it's better than most.

62384480
$50k cad customer support
36 yrs old
I am severely underperforming but I love making music -- married a woman from a well off family, we have a house
Confidence low, bros, confidence low

62384785
>>62384480
As it should be, you're the woman in the relationship.

62385873
>>62384785
Not exactly, but maybe to an extent as I am an artistic man. I'm definitely the defender though.

Not sure how to upgrade but also keep up with my artistry which I feel is my greatest passion and inspiration

At the very least I feel teaching your children music and related science opens up their mind to a lot of thinking

62385905
I make sandwitch

32 per hour aud 48 hr weeks

62388145
>>62381049
GSE Fleet mechanic for an airline 38$ an hour 60$ an hour for service calls

62388200
>>62381049
I'm 39m and a farmer. My dad retired about 10 years ago and I have taken over everything since then. Before then I had been working for him since I could walk pretty much. I remember the time before roundup ready beans and I spent most of my summers pulling weeds.

I made about $250k last year and my living expenses are about $10k. I live in town and own one of the cheapest houses in the county because my dad wouldn't let me live in a yurt in the grove. I drive a '78 F-150 I paid $400 for when I was in high school.

Anyways outside of planting and harvest I work about 3 hours per day and spend the rest of the day shitposting, checking my stocks, playing playstation 2, watching my sitcom DVD collection, and playing a round of golf at the local muni.

62388215
>>62381049
I make 60$ an hour to respond to passive aggressive emails
I got one waiting in my inbox rn.

62388275
$16 an hour at a dead end non-tech job even though I have a degree and some certs. I am the go to IT guy in my family which sometimes pays, the other day I installed a new hard drive and cleaned the pc for a family member. I didn't even ask for money since it was such a simple task but she gave me 60 bucks for it since that is what a store was going to charge her.

62388285
>>62381049
110k senior security consultant, wfh, unlimited pto, 4 years at the global company, burned out for the last 1.5 years but can't afford to quit because I got the job straight out of college through internship and I have no idea how to get another job because I refuse to do any certs or practice interviewing.

62388291
>>62388275
Why do you not have a job with your degree? Are you applying?

62388337
>>62388200
I envy the cheap pickups people can buy in farm country. on Long Island everything succumbs to rust almost immediately or is sold within the closed Spanish-speaking illegal community, so is impossible to buy.

62388396
>>62388291
Yea I've sent out over 350 applications. I've gotten my resume reviewed and edited by the alumni career services team, family members, and people in the industry. I have a home lab and document everything. I am not being picky and am willing to even take a job that pays less if it's in something tech related. Honestly it's gotten to the point where I'm close to giving up and just going to door knock for Andrew Yang so I get UBI.

62388428
39$/hour as a masonry laborer.

I started at 25$/hr in the company’s office, where I used my tech skills to build website stuff, learned and ran Google Ads and got some work for the company, which progressively rewarded me with raises.

I ran out of work at some point due to it being a small company so I was put in the field as a laborer. Now I do grunt work but get paid the same.

Although I’m not “growing”, this job feels like most feasible path for me to build my own construction company. I’ve already done jobs on the side and have my LLC open, website, SEO going on.

I want to scale my company to 10-15k/month net, but have been running into lots of cheap people when estimating jobs. It is a game of consistency, and sooner rather than later I can see my GBP with many reviews bringing me an organic pipeline of jobs, as well as eventual institutional/commercial contacts I meet here and there giving me consistent and higher margin work.

62388494
>>62381049
It ain't

62388513
>Consultant
I don't even care about the salary or the career as long as I can be there to axe working class workers.

62389047
>>62381049
I make 21$ an hour on welfare. 35k$ a year as a permaneet
I al thinking of buying an Ioniq 5 N-Line in gold
The reason I am ok the dole is because I have a booboo in my mental state snd alo a tiny, unused weenis

item.title: >boss put me on a PIP 2 months ago >been trying to find jobs a few months before that cuz I realized there was no pleasi… — 31 replies — 38.9/hr
item.content:encoded:
>boss put me on a PIP 2 months ago >been trying to find jobs a few months before that cuz I realized there was no pleasi…

Open thread • Replies: 31 • Updated: 2026-06-19 21:44 UTC

OP

>boss put me on a PIP 2 months ago
>been trying to find jobs a few months before that cuz I realized there was no pleasing him
>haven't found any jobs, its unreal how bad the market is, have this bad feeling its never going to get better
>PIP ends on Tuesday
I am an engineer. I started off as a tech and worked my ass off getting bachelors full time to escape manual labor. Now im gonna be that goy with a masters degree who took a 500 percent pay cut to do repetitive ligament destroying work. I honestly feel like god did this beacuse its too much bad things happening at once that are culminating into my total demise.

Early replies (first 3)

62388691
>>62388676
>500 percent pay cut to do repetitive ligament destroying work
Trades pay more than ever, especially vs pajeet-saturated office work. Skill issue

62388694
>>62388676
My entire life has been nothing but fighting tooth and nail, working myself 1000x harder than anyone anywhere ever for 0.001% of what anyone else gets for free. I have felt that god is purposely shitting on me pretty much my whole life. It doesn't get better, it only ever gets worse. If anything happens to me that seem to be a good thing, I have 6 months max to enjoy it because that'll be taken from me and then I'll be beaten up and crushed down for added effect. It's a real comedy out there.

So don't worry bro, you have a lot of time ahead of you to suffer far more. If there really is a god, he must be the most perfectly evil of entities.

62388697
The worst part is that in my meeting with my boss today he didnt mention if I will pass the PIP. He said he needed to talk with some people including HR. So 90 percent chance he is going to fire me but just enough chance he wont that I have to spend the weekend waiting instead of processing the pain. And if he doesnt fire me, I will be a totally buckbroken employee who will never care about a rasie and work 60 hours weeks. Like a absurd housewife that has no were else to go and she tries to esapce and gets rapped(applies to ghost jobs) even worse so she comes back to him. Thats me. I used to think my relationship with work was like between a man and a woman, were I am the man that picks and chooses women and is the one who leaves but its all turned around past couple of years. I am nothing but a side pieace female whore to these companies now, they treat me like a contract worker without paying me like one. They got us with not giving raises to match inflation, they got us with return to work mandates and now, they are going to get us by firing us and leaving us with nothing. U will get put on a PIP too, at the worst time possible also. They wont even lay u off anymore, they want to save a little bit of money by denying us unemployment insurance by firing us through a PIP.


Was being in ur 30s a good excperince back in the day? There is no way human civilization would have gotten to were it is if it was always this bad for everyone. Of course, I am a faggot and just suck but the fsct of the matter is that PIPs and no jobs has been a very common trope on the internet lately

Latest replies (last 30, oldest → newest)

62388702
>>62388694
Same thing with me with the 6 months to enjoy something, not a day more. I am realizing that we were supposed use those 6 months to prepare for the bad months instead of trying to enjoy it though. But all im asking from life is a steady job beacuse me and a lot of other people dont have any kind of support network or any kind.

62388706
>>62388691
Manual labor is significantly worse in every way. If i HAVE to do it then I understand but dont sit there and larp like its not a miserable way to live. Aslo they pay is terrible, u have been reading fake news

62388711
i got laid off two months ago

i get interviews for jobs in my domain but they are at the other side of europe (im eastern european)

i just bought an apartment and im indebted to the teeth, i still think i can go out of this trade at break even, no way im staying in this shithole any longer

all my ex-coworkers accepted low tier technical support / helpdesk / pajeet IT jobs but i refuse to do it because my gf would earn more than me

ah yes did i tell you? all women around me have fancy jobs with big salaries and 100% remote positions and company trips all over the world

62388729
>>62388697

You will still be working 60 hours a week but you will make money. Invest wisely so you can afford all the surgery you'll need in your 50s and 60s. I keep trying to force myself to look in my old field but even those jobs aren't hiring.

For some reason, I feel this will all pass when the orange retard is gone.

62388744
>>62388730
Ungrateful for what, nigger? Tell me ONE thing I should be grateful for. I got my work stolen, I got abandoned by everyone around me when they were contractually obliged and paid to support me in ways so bad I was ordered to cease all internal communication and contact an ombudsman, every achievement I fought for got ripped off from my hands, I've been explicitly told I was passed on because I am a white man and they need women instead, everytime I'm given the time of day people lose their mind about how hypercompetent I am (because I was forced to learn everything about everything to even so much as get said time of day, when others just waltz in no problem). At 32 I restarted my life from 0, moving to a foreign country I had no tie to and no knowledge of the language of because that was my only option to avoid homelessness and bankruptcy. I had $23.5 left in my bank account when I landed. I present with 500x (true number, no exaggeration) the work-related metrics of my peers and I'm told I should never even think of an internship because it's too low by a guy who got a fulltime lead position with 1/1000th of this metric.
Tell me one thing to be grateful about, nigger.

62388802
>>62388786
Surprisingly, very widely overblown and not at all in line with my experience. I see a nig or a nog here and there of course, but it's easily 95% white people in public. I know there are specific areas where the dregs tend to accumulate, but even then it's not that bad. I also learned the same applies to crime, when swedes say everything is dangerous and such, what they mean is they heard someone play music loudly once, or some man got mad and his girlfriend one night and they raised their voice a little. Of course it's true that crime stats show an increase in various crimes, but it's also very overblown in my experience at least.

62389085
>>62388697
>I will be a totally buckbroken employee who will never care about a rasie and work 60 hours weeks

do you have any money saved? if not, you let this happen to you. not to brag, but merely to show a contrasting approach. I worked for a bank and they tried to get me to sign a paper saying I acknowledged my annual performance wasn't good. I said no. they replied I could be put on a pip if I didn't. again, I replied no and I wouldn't sign a pip either. 2 weeks later they gave me a bonus check, still laugh thinking about their face when they told me. after I cashed the check, the day it cleared I emailed the manager that he was soft and technically incompetent. a week later HR let me go and again, gave me all of my PTO which was like 3 months living expenses for me. I tell you this to show you that having money allows you be confrontational and advocate for yourself. people can sniff weakness and poverty a mile away. I had my entire summer paid for, and very fortunately found a remote position in the fall. money attracts money.
